skill.name: chat-compactor
skill.description: Generate structured session summaries optimized for future AI agent consumption. Use when ending a coding session, user says 'compact' or 'summarize', context window is long, or before switching tasks.
skill.summary: Produces machine-readable handoff documents that preserve decision rationale, dead ends, and next steps for seamless session continuity.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' pattern matched markdown code fence backticks (```) not shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' finding at line 3 incorrectly flagged YAML frontmatter text. This skill contains only instructional markdown content with no executable code, network access, or security risks.

content.user_title: Generate Session Summaries for AI Continuity
content.value_statement: Lose critical context when sessions end. This skill produces structured handoff documents that preserve decisions, dead ends, and next steps for seamless AI agent continuity.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured markdown summaries with decision tables and rationale
Document dead ends and failed approaches to prevent re-exploration
Capture environment details, file changes, and gotchas for future reference
Create prioritized next steps lists with clear action items
Save session files with consistent naming conventions
Optimize summaries for quick scanning by AI agents
limitations: Requires access to full conversation history for accurate summarization
Cannot automatically save files to disk without user confirmation
Summary quality depends on conversation clarity and detail
Does not integrate with external project management or note-taking tools

use_cases.title: End-of-Session Handoff
use_cases.description: Developer finishing a debugging session generates a summary so the next AI session can immediately continue without re-explaining the problem context and attempted solutions.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using AI pair programming

use_cases.title: Long Project Continuity
use_cases.description: Team lead maintains project momentum across multiple AI sessions by creating structured handoff documents that preserve architectural decisions and implementation status.
use_cases.target_user: Project leads managing complex multi-session projects

use_cases.title: Context Window Management
use_cases.description: User proactively compacts lengthy conversations before the context window fills, preserving essential information while freeing up tokens for continued work.
use_cases.target_user: Power users working on extended AI-assisted tasks

prompt_templates.title: Basic Session Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Compact this session. Generate a structured summary with: what was accomplished, key decisions made, current state of the code, and next steps. Save as session-summary.md

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Handoff Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive handoff document for the next AI agent. Include decision tables with alternatives rejected, all modified files with change descriptions, dead ends that should not be retried, and environment gotchas discovered.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex debugging session with multiple decision points

prompt_templates.title: Mid-Session Context Checkpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a lightweight checkpoint summary before we continue. Capture current state, what's working, what's blocked, and immediate next steps. Keep it under 300 tokens.

prompt_templates.title: Project Transition Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: We're switching from feature A to feature B. Create a handoff document for feature A that captures: completed work, pending items, file locations, naming conventions established, and any quirks the next session should know.
prompt_templates.scenario: Task switching within a larger project

output_examples.input: User says: 'Let's wrap up, compact this session'
output: Summary includes: 3 key decisions with rationale, 2 dead ends documented, 5 modified files listed, 4 prioritized next steps

output_examples.input: User says: 'Save context before we switch to the API task'
output: Captured: React component structure, Tailwind configuration, build command, and deployment notes

best_practices: Document the 'why' behind decisions, not just the 'what' - future agents need rationale
Always list failed approaches in dead ends section to prevent wasted effort
Keep summaries scannable with clear headings, tables, and bullet points
anti_patterns: Writing narrative prose instead of structured, scannable content
Creating vague summaries like 'made good progress' without specific outcomes
Omitting failure documentation - dead ends are the most valuable content

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use at session end, when the user says 'compact' or 'summarize', before context switches, when context window exceeds 50%, or before taking breaks on ongoing work.

faq.question: Where are session files saved?
faq.answer: Sessions are saved to the project root or a designated sessions directory with naming format: session-[topic]-[date].md

faq.question: What makes a good session summary?
faq.answer: Good summaries are scannable in under 30 seconds, actionable with clear next steps, defensive by documenting dead ends, and minimal with no unnecessary content.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format?
faq.answer: Yes, the structured format is a template. You can request modifications like adding sections, changing table formats, or adjusting detail levels based on your needs.

faq.question: Should I create a summary for every session?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. Use for meaningful work sessions where continuity matters. Quick Q&A or standalone tasks typically don't need session documentation.

faq.question: How detailed should the dead ends section be?
faq.answer: Include enough detail to prevent retrying: what approach was tried, why it failed, and any error messages or symptoms. This prevents the most common AI re-exploration loops.
